title: Encephalartos pumilus
intro: Encephalartos pumilus is a species of cycad, a type of ancient, seed-producing plant that is often referred to as a "living fossil." This plant has been traditionally used in various parts of Africa for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, helping to alleviate conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Encephalartos pumilus include alkaloids, saponins, and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Encephalartos pumilus is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Encephalartos pumilus belongs to the family Zamiaceae, which is a group of cycads. Within this family, it is classified as part of the genus Encephalartos. It is further classified into the species Encephalartos pumilus, which is native to South Africa. The species Encephalartos pumilus is a small to medium-sized cycad with a distinctive appearance. It is typically found in well-drained, rocky areas in its native habitat.
